title: Fix parent-child tracker semantics that block child closure
description: br currently appears to treat parent-child relationships as hard blockers for child closure, even when the parent epic is active and the child work is independently complete. Reproduction in this project: after moving why-3vw.4 to in_progress and clearing its defer_until, br close why-3vw.4.4 and br close why-3vw.4.6 still fail with why-3vw.4:parent-blocked. Investigate whether this is intended beads behavior, stale blocked-cache behavior, or a project-graph modeling bug, and define the correct workflow for executable child tasks under roadmap epics.
notes: Confirmed findings: (1) clean ephemeral children with only a parent-child dependency can close successfully, even when the parent is deferred; (2) affected project tasks why-3vw.4.4 and why-3vw.4.6 remain blocked because blocked_issues_cache still stores why-3vw.4:parent-blocked after the parent epic was moved to in_progress and its deferral was cleared; (3) touching the child issues does not invalidate that cached blocked state. Current hypothesis: stale blocked_issues_cache invalidation for historical parent-blocked entries, not fundamental parent-child semantics.
close_reason: No current repro; historical stale blocked state no longer present in current graph

title: temp parent blocked repro epic
close_reason: Temporary repro epic no longer needed

title: Add representative large-history benchmark fixtures for cold-path validation
description: Create benchmark-oriented fixture repos and/or generators that approximate the performance targets described in PLAN.md section 11, including larger commit histories (for example ~1000-commit archaeology fixtures) and scanner-scalability scenarios. Use them to validate cold-cache latency and repo-wide scan targets rather than only tiny correctness fixtures with 2-3 commits.
close_reason: Added benchmark-only fixture scaling and fixed archaeology benchmark target wiring

title: Benchmark coupling and outage performance
Reconcile docs with implemented symbol-query support
description: Update README/POC/spec-style docs to match the actual Rust implementation, which already supports symbol queries across Rust, JavaScript, TypeScript, and Python. Clarify that symbol support is implemented even if richer synthesis/reporting phases remain future work, and align placeholder locator spec text accordingly.

title: temp parent blocked repro child
close_reason: Repro child closes after parent unblocked

title: Exclude vendored trees from repo-wide scanners
description: Repo-wide scanner validation on the why repo shows  and  fail because they recurse into vendored  and then hit git-blame errors on those files.  also runs far slower than plan targets on the full repo. Add source-scope exclusions or git-tracked filtering so scanners avoid vendored/non-project trees such as node_modules and similar generated/vendor content.

title: Deliver the why implementation roadmap from PLAN.md
description: Translate PLAN.md into an execution-ready bead graph with phase epics, granular implementation tasks, explicit dependencies, and enough context that future contributors can understand not only what to build, but why the sequence and scope choices matter.
close_reason: Planned roadmap complete; future backlog remains deferred

comments.author: claude
comments.text:
This root epic exists to turn a very large design document into an operational roadmap.

Decomposition principles used here:
1. Mirror the implementation phases in PLAN.md so the bead graph preserves the narrative and intended rollout order.
2. Separate foundational query-path work from integrations and repo-wide scanners so the core value proposition can land early.
3. Preserve cross-cutting concerns as first-class work: testing, performance, error handling, security, CI/CD, packaging, and release readiness.
4. Capture future enhancements as explicit backlog issues rather than leaving them trapped in prose.

Future-self guidance:
- Prefer closing work in phase order unless there is a clear reason to pull something forward.
- Keep the CLI query pipeline correct before optimizing secondary surfaces like hooks, LSP, or shell UX.
- Treat the testing and hardening epics as non-optional product work, not polish. The trustworthiness of why is core to the product.

comments.author: claude
comments.text: Planning revision: the root roadmap epic is deferred intentionally so `br ready` highlights real executable work instead of a meta-container. Treat this issue as program context, not an implementation step.

title: Validate the concept with a Node.js proof of concept
description: Validate that git history plus LLM synthesis produces useful archaeological explanations before the Rust workspace absorbs the full implementation cost.

comments.author: claude
comments.text:
Why this phase matters: the whole product thesis is that commit history plus local code context can answer why code exists in a way developers actually trust.

The POC should aggressively test that thesis on representative targets, because a wrong product thesis would make a polished Rust implementation expensive but unconvincing.

This phase should optimize for learning speed, not code quality or permanence.

comments.author: claude
comments.text: Planning revision: this epic is deferred as a container. Work should normally be claimed from its child tasks so the ready queue surfaces executable work rather than umbrella planning nodes.

title: Implement the end-to-end Node.js archaeology prototype
description: Build the simple-git plus Anthropic script that can gather file history, assemble a prompt, and emit structured explanations for selected targets.
close_reason: Prototype implemented and aligned with documented raw evidence flow

title: Curate representative validation targets and scenarios
description: Select real functions and scenarios with varied history shapes such as hotfixes, compatibility shims, migrations, and routine utility code so evaluation covers both strong-signal and weak-signal cases.

title: Evaluate prototype usefulness and record go or no-go findings
description: Review prototype output quality for accuracy, actionability, and confidence calibration, then capture the product learnings that should shape the Rust implementation.
close_reason: Evaluation findings and product learnings recorded in POC

title: Define the MVP success rubric and evaluation template for the POC
description: Specify how prototype outputs will be judged for accuracy, actionability, confidence calibration, and evidence grounding so the go/no-go decision is repeatable rather than intuitive.

comments.author: claude
comments.text: This task exists because the original graph validated the concept without first defining what success means. A prototype can feel impressive while still failing the actual user-value test. The rubric should include examples of acceptable and unacceptable explanations, expected confidence behavior under thin evidence, and what would count as strong enough signal to justify the Rust build.

title: Ship phase 9 repo-wide analysis scanners
description: Expand from single-target archaeology to whole-repository risk discovery with scanners that find stale debt, hotspots, onboarding priorities, and risky diffs.

comments.author: claude
comments.text:
Repo-wide analysis is how why graduates from a point tool into a codebase intelligence layer.

These scanners should reuse the core query pipeline rather than inventing parallel logic. The point is to amplify the same evidence model across a broader surface area.

title: Implement time-bomb scanning with dated TODO parsing and blame-based aging
description: Find stale markers and deferred cleanup items whose age or due date now makes them operationally dangerous.
close_reason: Implemented time-bombs CLI command with --age-days and --json flags. Added terminal rendering function, CLI parsing, tests, and updated help text.

title: Implement hotspots scanning using churn x risk scoring
description: Rank the files where change frequency and risk-sensitive context overlap so teams can see where accidental complexity is accumulating.
close_reason: Validated existing hotspots scanner and CLI coverage

title: Implement onboarding report generation for the top N must-understand symbols
description: Identify and narrate the historically important symbols a new engineer should understand before making changes.
close_reason: Validated existing onboarding scanner and CLI coverage

title: Implement diff review and optional GitHub PR comment flow
description: Assess touched functions in a branch or PR range, summarize risk, and optionally publish the result back to GitHub.

title: Create scanner fixture repos for time-bomb and coupling scenarios
description: Build deterministic repositories that exercise time-bomb detection and scanner-level coupling analysis for regression coverage.
close_reason: Validated dedicated rich fixture repos and focused regression coverage

title: Ship phase 10 integrations: MCP, hooks, annotator
description: Expose the why pipeline to AI tools and developer workflows through MCP, git hooks, and source-level annotations.
close_reason: All phase 10 child beads are closed and validated

comments.author: claude
comments.text:
These integrations matter because the highest-value version of why is ambient. It should be easy for humans and coding agents to consult history before risky edits.

The integration surfaces must stay inspectable, local-first, and safe by default so teams trust them in day-to-day development.

comments.author: claude
comments.text: Planning revision: integrations should land behind a functioning CI baseline. MCP, hooks, and annotations amplify usage quickly, so they need automated quality gates in place first.

title: Implement the MCP stdio server and tool dispatch surface
description: Wrap core capabilities as MCP tools over stdio so Claude Code and other compatible assistants can call them directly.
close_reason: Validated complete: MCP/docs/annotator integration surfaces are implemented and focused tests/docs review passed

title: Document MCP setup for Claude Code, Cursor, and Neovim
description: Provide copy-paste configuration and behavioral guidance so teams can adopt the server without reverse-engineering the setup.
close_reason: Validated complete: MCP/docs/annotator integration surfaces are implemented and focused tests/docs review passed

title: Implement git hook install and uninstall with backup and restore semantics
description: Create pre-commit and pre-push hook flows that warn about risky edits without becoming fragile or opaque.

title: Implement source annotation writing and idempotent refresh behavior
description: Write short evidence-backed why annotations into source files in a way that can be refreshed safely.
close_reason: Validated complete: MCP/docs/annotator integration surfaces are implemented and focused tests/docs review passed

title: Add integration coverage for MCP tools, hook installation, and annotation flows
description: Verify the integration surfaces behave correctly in realistic end-to-end scenarios rather than only in unit isolation.
close_reason: Validated complete: MCP/docs/annotator integration surfaces are implemented and focused tests/docs review passed

title: Ship phase 11 extended scanners: health, ghost, coverage, outage, PR template
description: Add the higher-order scanner features that synthesize repository health, dead-but-dangerous code, coverage gaps, incident archaeology, and risk-aware PR drafting.
close_reason: All phase 11 child beads are closed and validated

comments.author: claude
comments.text:
This phase turns scanner output into management and operational insight.

The unifying idea is prioritization: if a repository is too large to understand manually, why should identify the most dangerous debt, the most brittle gaps, and the most informative historical signals to act on first.

title: Implement health dashboard aggregation and trend snapshots
description: Aggregate scanner outputs into a debt score with trend history so teams can monitor whether repository risk is improving or regressing.
close_reason: Completed; implementation already landed in commits 394d6c3 and 71f1631

title: Implement ghost detection with static-call heuristics and clear disclaimers
description: Find functions that appear uncalled yet carry high-risk history, while making the static-analysis limitations explicit.
close_reason: Validated complete: phase 11 ghost/coverage/pr-template/fixture coverage is implemented and focused tests passed

title: Implement coverage-gap analysis for LCOV and llvm-cov JSON
description: Cross-reference historical risk with coverage data so teams can focus tests where failure would matter most.
close_reason: Validated complete: phase 11 ghost/coverage/pr-template/fixture coverage is implemented and focused tests passed

title: Implement outage archaeology over a bounded incident time window
description: Enumerate suspicious commits in an outage window and rank likely culprits using risk, recency, and blast-radius signals.
close_reason: Wired explain-outage CLI mode to the outage scanner with ISO-8601 window parsing and tests

title: Implement PR template generation and health --ci exit behavior
description: Generate reviewer-friendly risk-aware PR text and allow CI to fail when debt thresholds are exceeded.
close_reason: Validated complete: phase 11 ghost/coverage/pr-template/fixture coverage is implemented and focused tests passed

title: Create ghost and split fixture repos and scanner regression coverage
description: Add deterministic repositories that validate ghost detection, split suggestions, and related scanner behaviors.
close_reason: Validated complete: phase 11 ghost/coverage/pr-template/fixture coverage is implemented and focused tests passed

title: Ship phase 12 LSP, shell, splitter, context inject, and custom vocab polish
description: Complete the editor, interactive, and ambient-assistance surfaces that make archaeological context available without leaving the coding flow.
close_reason: All phase 12 child beads are closed and validated

comments.author: claude
comments.text:
This phase is about distribution of context.

Once the core reasoning is trustworthy, the product should meet developers where they already work: in hover tooltips, in REPL-like exploration, and in ambient wrappers around AI tools. Latency and ergonomics matter as much as raw capability here.

comments.author: claude
comments.text: Planning revision: editor and ambient-assistance surfaces are intentionally gated behind CI maturity so latency-sensitive UX and integration behavior do not regress silently.

title: Implement the LSP hover server using the fast heuristic path
description: Provide low-latency hover summaries that surface risk and short history without depending on network calls.
close_reason: Validated complete: LSP/shell/context-inject/config flows are implemented and focused tests/docs review passed

title: Implement the interactive shell with file and symbol completion
description: Support iterative archaeology sessions that keep repository state warm and make exploratory use dramatically faster.
close_reason: Validated complete: LSP/shell/context-inject/config flows are implemented and focused tests/docs review passed

title: Implement splitter clustering, era labeling, and extract-name suggestions
description: Use line-level history to suggest historically coherent function boundaries for safer refactors.
close_reason: Validated complete: splitter implementation and focused integration coverage are passing

title: Implement context-inject shell wrappers for AI tooling
description: Generate wrappers that prepend archaeology output to supported AI commands so historical context appears by default.
close_reason: Validated complete: LSP/shell/context-inject/config flows are implemented and focused tests/docs review passed

description: Make team-specific risk vocabularies and configuration discoverable so the product adapts cleanly across domains.
close_reason: Validated complete: LSP/shell/context-inject/config flows are implemented and focused tests/docs review passed

description: Exercise editor and shell surfaces end-to-end to protect latency-sensitive UX from regressions.
close_reason: Validated complete: LSP/shell/context-inject/config flows are implemented and focused tests/docs review passed

title: Establish comprehensive testing, fixtures, and benchmark coverage
description: Turn the test strategy in the plan into executable coverage that protects correctness, confidence calibration, performance targets, and regression detection across the whole product.

comments.author: claude
comments.text:
Testing is part of the product promise.

Because why is an evidence and risk explanation tool, small regressions in parsing, ranking, or synthesis quality can silently erode trust. The fixture corpus and benchmark suite should be treated as product assets, not just engineering scaffolding.

comments.author: claude
comments.text: Planning revision: the testing epic no longer waits on phase 1 as a whole. Test-harness and fixture-design work can and should begin in parallel with early implementation so contract drift is caught earlier.

comments.author: claude
comments.text: Planning revision: this epic is deferred as a container. Claim fixture-design, harness, unit, snapshot, or benchmark tasks directly.

title: Build the reusable fixture harness and shared integration-test helpers
description: Create the common test setup layer so scripted repositories and command execution helpers can be reused consistently across modules and scanner suites.
close_reason: Restoring closed status after accidental reopen

title: Implement the unit-test matrix across locator, archaeologist, context, evidence, synthesizer, and cache
description: Translate the detailed unit-test inventory from the plan into real test cases that lock down parsing, ranking, heuristics, and cache correctness.

title: Add snapshot and golden coverage for terminal and JSON outputs
description: Record expected user-facing and JSON outputs for fixture repositories so formatting and semantic regressions are caught early.

title: Add criterion benches and performance regression checks
description: Benchmark the most important hot paths and make the response-time targets in the plan observable rather than aspirational.

title: Validate warm and cold latency targets plus scanner scalability goals
description: Measure the implemented system against the explicit latency and throughput targets from the plan and record any gaps that need follow-up.
close_reason: Validated current benchmarks against plan targets; identified follow-up gap tracked in why-25o

title: Design the fixture corpus and golden-output strategy before broad implementation
description: Define which archaeology scenarios deserve first-class fixtures, how snapshots should be organized, and which outputs are contract-level versus presentation-level so the test suite grows coherently.

comments.author: claude
comments.text: This task is pulled earlier than pure implementation because fixture strategy is architecture. The right corpus shapes module boundaries, output stability expectations, and which edge cases the team treats as product-critical.

title: Harden error handling, security posture, CI/CD, and release readiness
description: Implement the cross-cutting operational work that makes the product safe, diagnosable, automatable, and releasable.
close_reason: All hardening child beads are closed and validated

comments.author: claude
comments.text:
This epic protects the credibility of the tool outside the happy path.

If errors are opaque, secrets leak into logs, CI coverage is incomplete, or release mechanics are fuzzy, the product will be difficult to trust and difficult to adopt even if the core analysis works well.

comments.author: claude
comments.text: Planning revision: hardening is not purely post-LLM work. Error taxonomy, redaction rules, and CI scaffolding should begin alongside core implementation, with integration-specific hardening still blocked where appropriate.

comments.author: claude
comments.text: Planning revision: this epic is deferred as a container. Claim hardening tasks directly so triage reflects concrete deliverables.

title: Implement crate-local error types and meaningful exit-code semantics
description: Define explicit user-facing error surfaces and exit-code behavior so failures guide operators toward the next correct action.

title: Harden secret handling, cache safety, and local-only integration boundaries
description: Ensure tokens stay out of logs and cache artifacts, preserve stdio-only trust boundaries for integrations, and document any insecure config escape hatches clearly.
close_reason: Validated complete: repo-local config boundaries, cache symlink safety, and GitHub token redaction tests are passing

title: Implement CI workflows for formatting, clippy, tests, snapshots, audit, bench, and PR diff review
description: Codify the workflows from the plan so every important quality gate is automatically exercised in pull requests and on main.

title: Define release, installation, and crates.io readiness checklists
description: Document and automate the path from local development to installable public artifact so adoption does not depend on tribal knowledge.
close_reason: Aligned release-readiness surfaces by replacing the remaining code-side why-cli package-name mismatch in the GitHub user-agent path and removing the now-stale README checklist bullet

title: Track near-term enhancements beyond phase 12
description: Capture the concrete post-v2 enhancements that naturally extend the current architecture but should not distract from landing the planned roadmap first.

comments.author: claude
comments.text:
These items are intentionally explicit backlog entries so they remain visible without polluting the critical path.

The common thread is leverage: each feature extends existing foundations rather than requiring a full product reset.

title: Add backlog item for multi-language expansion and batch-mode queries
description: Extend target coverage to more tree-sitter languages and support multiple targets per invocation once the single-target pipeline is stable.
delete_reason: Obsolete issue text no longer matches current code state

title: Track remaining GitLab integration work
description: Broaden hosting-platform support and improve CI enforcement by watching debt-score deltas, not only absolute thresholds.
notes: Audit on 2026-03-15: health regression CI gates are implemented in .github/workflows/ci.yml, but GitLab integration is not implemented. GitLab remotes are still rejected by parse_github_remote in crates/evidence/src/lib.rs.
close_reason: GitLab support is not planned; keep health regression work covered elsewhere

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text: Implemented GitHub Actions health regression gating with a checked-in baseline at .github/health-baseline.json. CI now runs why health with max-regression=0 plus per-signal zero budgets, and pull requests prefer the base branch baseline when it can be fetched.

comments.author: quangdang
comments.text: Reopened: Closed status no longer matches current code after audit

title: Track remaining watch mode and rename-safe work
description: Improve interactive usability with live updates, safer rename workflows, and richer terminal affordances.
notes: Audit on 2026-03-15: clickable terminal links are implemented via OSC 8 in crates/core/src/main.rs, and rename-aware evolution exists via --evolution, but there is still no watch mode or dedicated rename-safe caller-risk workflow.

comments.author: quangdang
comments.text: Reopened: Closed status no longer matches current code after audit

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text: GrayGrove: aligned rename-safe integration coverage with current behavior. Updated MCP tools/list expectation for why_rename_safe, added/updated rename-safe MCP golden coverage, and corrected CLI/MCP rename-safe assertions to match current caller ordering and null qualified_name for free function callers. Validated with: cargo test --test integration_mcp; cargo test --test integration_cli rename_safe_queries_return_ranked_json_for_fixture_repo; cargo test --test integration_cli rename_safe_queries_render_terminal_output_for_fixture_repo.

title: Expose qualified symbol inventory for rename-safe workflows
description: Add a locator-side symbol inventory API that exposes each symbol's qualified name (when known) alongside its line range so rename-safe and other scanner workflows can present caller identities without reimplementing tree-sitter symbol metadata extraction.

comments.author: PearlCat
comments.text: Scoped a complementary sub-slice after coordination: avoid FoggyCove’s reserved core/rename_safe files and focus on reusable scanner-side caller discovery helpers on non-overlapping files.

title: Add watch mode coverage for interactive rendering
description: Add focused test coverage for the existing --watch workflow in why-core. Cover watch frame rendering and file-state change tracking without overlapping existing rename-safe work. Keep scope to current behavior rather than inventing new UX.

title: Track PR metadata enrichment from GitHub refs
description: Implement a narrow near-term slice of README roadmap item 'GitHub/GitLab PR title + description integration': enrich GitHub issue/PR refs with pull request metadata when the referenced number is a PR, preserving current issue behavior. Keep scope to GitHub only for now and add focused tests.

title: Track mid-term and long-term product expansion bets
description: Preserve the larger strategic ideas from the plan so the team can revisit them deliberately once the core platform proves itself in real usage.
delete_reason: Removed strategic backlog branch per user request

comments.author: claude
comments.text:
These are not implementation commitments; they are memory anchors for strategic options.

They belong in the graph because they explain where the architecture may eventually stretch: from single-repo archaeology toward institutional memory, cross-repo reasoning, and richer organizational intelligence.

title: Add backlog item for VS Code extension and GitHub Actions app distribution
description: Package the editor and automation integrations into more turnkey distribution forms once the underlying services are mature.
delete_reason: Removed strategic backlog branch per user request

title: Add backlog item for teach-mode, culture reports, and team-scoped hotspot views
description: Layer explicit human domain context and organizational reporting on top of the git archaeology substrate.
delete_reason: Removed strategic backlog branch per user request

title: Add backlog item for multi-repo reasoning and architectural memory maps
description: Extend the model beyond a single repository so teams can trace historical intent across service boundaries and over time.
delete_reason: Removed strategic backlog branch per user request

title: Add backlog item for semantic similarity and automatic incident linkage
description: Use higher-level similarity and incident correlation to recover historical purpose even when code has been renamed or heavily rewritten.
delete_reason: Removed strategic backlog branch per user request

title: Ship phase 1 CLI and line-targeting MVP
description: Stand up a working Rust binary that can resolve a line target, run git blame, and show commit-backed output without any LLM dependency.

comments.author: claude
comments.text:
This is the first real product slice: a developer can point at a line and immediately see the commits most responsible for it.

The intent is to prove the non-LLM substrate first: repository discovery, targeting, blame, commit enrichment, and terminal output. Everything later depends on this path being correct and debuggable.

title: Scaffold the Cargo workspace and crate boundaries
description: Create the multi-crate workspace skeleton described in the plan so each concern has a clear home and later phases do not accumulate accidental coupling in the CLI layer.

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text: Completed workspace scaffolding: added root Cargo workspace, placeholder root crate for test harness compatibility, core binary crate, and stub library crates for planned architecture. Validated with cargo fmt, cargo check, cargo test, and UBS on changed Rust files.

title: Implement clap CLI parsing and query command dispatch
description: Define the initial CLI surface for file:line and explicit range queries, wire global flags such as --json and --no-llm, and keep business logic out of the entrypoint.

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text: Implemented the phase-1 clap CLI surface in the core crate: positional file:line targets, <file> --lines <start:end>, --json, and --no-llm. Added unit coverage for valid line/range parsing and explicit rejection of symbol syntax until phase 2, plus placeholder dispatch output for the next archaeology bead.

title: Implement repository discovery and blame-range collection
description: Discover the repo root, run git blame against a bounded line range, and return the unique commit identifiers that own the target lines.

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text: Implemented repository discovery and bounded line-range blame in the archaeologist crate using git2::Repository::discover and git2 blame. Wired the phase-1 CLI to resolve repo-relative paths, collect unique commit OIDs for line/range queries, and emit the raw archaeology JSON/terminal output that phase 1 expects.

title: Render commit-backed terminal and JSON output for line queries
description: Load basic commit metadata and present the results in both human-readable terminal form and machine-readable JSON so downstream tests and integrations have stable output surfaces.

title: Freeze the phase 1 CLI and output contract before implementation spreads
description: Write down the precise phase-1 command forms, output fields, and JSON shape so early implementation and tests converge on a stable user contract.

comments.author: claude
comments.text: This is a plan-space optimization task. It reduces churn by making the first shippable CLI slice explicit before multiple crates start coding against slightly different assumptions about flags, output wording, and JSON structure.

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text: Froze the phase-1 CLI contract in project docs: positional line/range targeting only, --json as the machine-readable phase-1 surface, and explicit separation between Node POC commands and the Rust shipping interface. Updated PLAN.md, README.md, POC.md, and locator test notes to reduce implementation drift before CLI work starts.

title: Ship phase 2 symbol resolution with tree-sitter
description: Upgrade from line-only archaeology to symbol-aware targeting across supported languages so the tool can answer why a function, type, or method exists.

comments.author: claude
comments.text:
Symbol resolution is a major usability unlock. Developers think in functions, methods, classes, and types, not just line numbers.

This phase should keep correctness ahead of breadth: a narrower set of languages with dependable targeting is more valuable than broad but flaky symbol resolution.

title: Implement target parsing for file:line, file:symbol, qualified names, and --lines
description: Support every target shape in the plan and return a normalized internal representation that later resolution logic can consume consistently.

title: Implement language detection and query-loading infrastructure
description: Map file extensions to supported tree-sitter grammars and load the correct query definitions so symbol search remains modular as language coverage expands.

title: Add Rust symbol resolution for functions, impl methods, and core type definitions
description: Resolve Rust-specific constructs accurately, including impl methods and common declaration forms that dominate the initial target use case.

title: Add TypeScript and JavaScript symbol resolution
description: Support function declarations, arrow functions, class methods, and classes in JS and TS so the tool covers common mixed-language repositories.

title: Add Python symbol resolution, ambiguity warnings, and list_all_symbols support
description: Support Python defs and classes, warn clearly on ambiguous matches, and expose symbol enumeration needed by shell and ghost-analysis features later on.

title: Ship phase 3 evidence, context, and heuristic risk engine
description: Assemble the high-signal evidence that explains code history even without an LLM and compute a trustworthy heuristic risk level from commit metadata and local code context.
close_reason: All phase-3 child tasks are complete in current graph

comments.author: claude
comments.text:
This phase builds the product's evidence discipline.

The goal is not merely to gather more data, but to gather the right data: issue references, diff excerpts, mechanical-commit detection, context markers, and bounded evidence packs. This is the layer that makes later LLM synthesis grounded instead of decorative.

title: Enrich commit loading with diffs, issue references, and mechanical-commit detection
description: Expand commit metadata so the system can distinguish meaningful historical reasons from noise such as formatting, merges, and bulk refactors.

title: Implement commit relevance scoring and top-N selection
description: Turn raw history into a ranked set of commits that maximize explanatory value for a bounded synthesis budget.

title: Implement local context extraction and heuristic risk classification
description: Scan the code neighborhood for comments, TODO or HACK markers, and risk-sensitive vocabulary so the tool remains useful even when offline or unauthenticated.

title: Load .why.toml configuration and custom risk vocabulary
description: Support domain-specific terms and tuning knobs so teams can teach the heuristic layer what counts as risky in their environment.

title: Build the bounded evidence-pack payload for synthesis and fallback mode
description: Compress target metadata, ranked history, and local signals into a size-bounded structure that can power both the heuristic output and later LLM calls.

title: Define the user-facing risk semantics and explanation style guide
description: Make HIGH, MEDIUM, and LOW meanings operational for users and define the wording style for explanations, evidence, unknowns, and confidence so outputs stay trustworthy and legible.

comments.author: claude
comments.text: The original plan describes risk heuristics and prompt rules, but a dedicated issue makes the product language an explicit design artifact. This should answer: what does HIGH obligate the user to do, what claims belong in evidence vs unknowns, and what kinds of wording would overstate certainty.

title: Ship phase 4 LLM synthesis and WhyReport generation
description: Turn evidence packs into concise, structured WhyReports while preserving strict grounding, fallback behavior, and cost visibility.

comments.author: claude
comments.text:
The LLM layer should summarize and synthesize, not invent.

This phase should enforce a strict output contract, make inference quality legible, and degrade gracefully when the model or key is unavailable. The product must still be useful without a network call.

title: Codify the WhyReport schema and prompt contract
description: Define the exact structured response shape and the anti-hallucination rules that make the model output parseable and reviewable.

title: Implement the Anthropic client with retries, timeout handling, and cost accounting
description: Call the Anthropic Messages API reliably, back off on transient failures, and expose enough accounting to show approximate per-query cost.

title: Parse model responses and implement heuristic fallback generation
description: Convert raw model text into WhyReport values, normalize variants safely, and provide a deterministic fallback when parsing or credentials fail.

title: Integrate terminal formatter and JSON serialization for WhyReport output
description: Present summaries, risk, evidence, confidence, unknowns, and cost consistently so the human UX and machine API stay aligned.

title: Ship phase 5 cache layer and invalidation model
description: Cache expensive query results with correctness-preserving invalidation so repeated analysis feels instantaneous without serving stale answers after history changes.

comments.author: claude
comments.text:
Caching is not optional performance polish here. It is the mechanism that makes the tool pleasant enough to live in a developer feedback loop.

The key design constraint is correctness: cache invalidation should ride on HEAD-sensitive keys and bounded entry management so users do not have to reason about manual cache clearing.

title: Implement cache storage, keying, eviction, and entry serialization
description: Build the on-disk cache with HEAD-aware keys, bounded retention, and robust read-write behavior.

title: Wire cache lookup, refresh, --no-cache, and cached-hit indicators into query flow
description: Integrate caching into the main query path so hits are immediate, misses refresh cleanly, and debugging paths remain explicit.

title: Support health snapshots and document .why cache semantics
description: Persist historical health snapshots and document the .why directory behavior, ignore rules, and operator expectations.

title: Ship phase 6 GitHub enrichment and rename-aware history
description: Enrich local git evidence with PR and issue context while improving historical continuity across renamed or moved files.

comments.author: claude
comments.text:
GitHub enrichment raises answer quality when commit subjects alone are too thin to explain intent.

The feature should remain additive rather than mandatory: repositories without a token must still work, just with lower confidence and less surrounding context.

title: Implement GitHub API client and authentication/config surface
description: Add the API plumbing, token handling, and remote configuration needed to hydrate local issue and PR references safely.
close_reason: Validated implemented GitHub config and client surface

title: Hydrate PR and issue references into richer evidence-pack context
description: Resolve linked items from commit messages into titles and descriptions that can materially improve synthesis quality and confidence.

title: Handle rate limits and graceful degradation for optional GitHub data
description: Respect rate-limit signals, avoid hard failures, and surface clearly when the answer is operating with thinner evidence.

title: Implement rename-aware log traversal for moved files and symbols
description: Extend history collection beyond the current pathname so archaeology remains useful after file moves and structural cleanup work.

title: Ship phase 7 polish, packaging, and ergonomics
description: Round out the core product with history-scoping controls, team-awareness reporting, and installable packaging that makes the tool easier to adopt broadly.
close_reason: Completed; all child tasks why-3vw.8.1..8.4 and upstream blocker why-3vw.7 are already closed

comments.author: claude
comments.text:
This phase turns the core from an interesting engineering artifact into a tool people can actually install, understand, and fit into daily work.

The main theme is operator ergonomics: better history controls, bus-factor visibility, packaging, manuals, and shell completions.

title: Add --since, --team, and better merge-origin handling
description: Expose history-window control, team ownership reporting, and more reliable origin handling for merge-heavy repositories.

title: Prepare cargo install, man pages, and shell completion artifacts
description: Make the binary feel like a normal CLI product with installation, command discovery, and completion support.
close_reason: Implemented first-class CLI artifact generation via why completions <bash|zsh|fish> and why manpage, documented local artifact generation in README, and validated with why-core build/tests.

title: Document CLAUDE.md snippets and user-facing adoption guidance
description: Teach teams how to operationalize why in interactive coding flows and code-review routines.

title: Review user workflow friction after the core query path is real
description: Pause after the core query pipeline exists and reassess whether command shape, report structure, and latency characteristics actually support the intended developer workflow before expanding surfaces.
close_reason: Resolved by completed workflow-friction review: CLI help now communicates core workflows, thread findings captured command-shape/report-density concerns, and downstream packaging/adoption tasks can proceed from that product judgment.

comments.author: claude
comments.text: This issue exists because it is cheap to change ergonomics in plan space and early implementation space, but expensive once MCP, hooks, LSP, shell, and docs all encode the first design. Use it to revisit command naming, output density, confidence presentation, and whether key workflows feel obvious.

title: Ship phase 8 advanced archaeology: blame chain, coupling, evolution
description: Go beyond point-in-time explanations by recovering true origins through noise, detecting co-change relationships, and narrating how targets evolved over time.
close_reason: All sub-tasks (why-3vw.9.1, .9.2, .9.3, .9.4) are closed. Phase 8 advanced archaeology is complete.

comments.author: claude
comments.text:
This phase deepens archaeological credibility.

Developers often need more than a snapshot answer. They need to know which commits were just noise, which files always move together, and how a function accumulated today's risk profile over time.

title: Implement blame-chain traversal past mechanical commits
description: Walk parent commits to skip formatting or bulk-refactor noise and surface the commit that best explains why the code exists in the first place.

title: Implement co-change coupling analysis with ratio-based ranking
description: Analyze commit co-occurrence so the tool can warn when editing one function or file likely implies touching another.

title: Implement log-based symbol evolution and rename-following history collection
description: Reconstruct a target's major eras from historical changes so narrative evolution output has grounded raw material.

title: Implement evolution-mode synthesis and timeline presentation
description: Turn era snapshots into a concise timeline that explains inflection points, risk escalation, and possible refactoring boundaries.
close_reason: Validated evolution timeline CLI/output is implemented via existing code, tests, and golden coverage

title: Audit and reconcile stale parent-blocked child beads
description: Investigate beads that appear already implemented in the repo but cannot be closed because their deferred parent epic marks them parent-blocked. Determine whether this is intended workflow, tracker misuse, or a state-sync problem; document findings; and reconcile issue status or file follow-up beads accordingly. Initial examples: why-3vw.4.4 and why-3vw.4.6.

title: Normalize deferred epic statuses when child work is active
description: Audit and fix tracker workflow where deferred parent epics hard-block child tasks that are already open or in progress. Identify deferred epics with active children, decide whether they should be moved to in_progress or otherwise re-modeled, and reconcile the graph so completed child tasks can close cleanly. Rooted in findings from why-jt0.
close_reason: Current graph normalized; no deferred parent with active children found

title: temp parent blocked repro blocker
Audit WhyReport contract drift against PLAN and implementation
description: Compare PLAN.md phase-4 WhyReport contract with the current crates/synthesizer and crates/core implementation, identify any contract drift or missing fields, and capture the follow-up as a concrete bead so the LLM output surface stays explicit and reviewable.
